commit.message:
Remove Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy header to unblock YouTube embeds (#343)

* Initial plan

* fix: remove Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy header to unblock YouTube iframe embeds

COEP (credentialless) blocked cross-origin iframes like YouTube when
using react-player. Since OPFS SAH Pool VFS does not require
SharedArrayBuffer, the COEP header is unnecessary. The COOP header
(same-origin) is retained as a security measure.

Update src/endpoints/home/homeEvents.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement R_X86_64_TLSGD relocation with GOT entry management

Completes ROADMAP Section 7.3 TLS Support item: R_X86_64_TLSGD

Adds full support for General Dynamic TLS model code sequence relocations:
- GOT entry allocation infrastructure with lazy page allocation
- allocateGOTEntryPair() manages 16-byte GOT entry pairs for TLS symbols
- applyTlsgd() populates GOT with DTPMOD64/DTPOFF64 and patches PC-relative offset
- symAddress() helper to retrieve symbol values for TLS offset calculation

GOT management uses a single 4KB page for entry storage with per-symbol
tracking via GOTEntries map. Entries are reused across multiple relocations
to the same symbol.

While modern compilers (GCC 14+) default to DTPMOD64/DTPOFF64 relocations
(which were already supported), this implementation ensures compatibility
with older TLS dialects that generate TLSGD code sequences.

All existing tests pass. ROADMAP updated to mark TLSGD as complete.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

When --source-dir is a relative subdirectory (e.g., Inbox/), cache should
still be loaded from the vault root to maintain consistency with other cache

Addresses Copilot review comment.

- fix(fetch): output JSON on RSS error for structured error handling
- fix(move): remove exists=True validation and add manual check after normalization
- test(fetcher): improve malformed encoding test to reflect lenient unquote behavior

* fix(cache): handle absolute cache paths correctly in filter_duplicate_urls and update_cache_after_fetch

* fix: address final Copilot PR review comments

- fix(fetcher): make percent-encoded URL pattern case-insensitive
- fix(fetcher): remove broad exception handler from unquote (not needed)
- fix(fetcher): simplify HTTPError exception handling (RequestError is subclass)

- fix(move): add is_dir() check after exists() to reject file paths passed as --source-dir
- fix(fetch): use click.echo for JSON output to avoid Rich line-wrapping
- fix(duplicates): use click.echo for structured output to avoid Rich line-wrapping
- fix(stats): use click.echo for JSON/YAML output to avoid Rich line-wrapping

* fix: address Copilot review comments and proactively fix similar patterns

commit.message:
chore: add go.work to support multi-module workspace (#1618)

Running `go mod tidy` in the root inadvertently affects `website/go.mod`
and `website/go.sum` because the Go toolchain doesn't recognize the
nested module without an explicit workspace declaration. This also
breaks `gopls` language server support across both modules.

## Changes

- **Add `go.work`** at the repository root declaring both modules as
workspace members:

```go
go 1.24.0

use (
    .
    ./website
)
```

This scopes `go mod tidy` to each module independently and enables
correct multi-module analysis in editors. For website dependency updates
(e.g. Docsy), use `hugo mod tidy` / `hugo mod get -u` inside `website/`
rather than `go mod tidy`.

fix: prevent URL navigation when RiSc deletion requires approval (#905)

When deleting a RiSc that requires approval, the onSuccess callback
(which navigates to the first RiSc) was always called regardless of
whether the deletion was complete or merely sent for approval.

This caused the URL to immediately change to the first RiSc even though
the deleted RiSc still existed in the list with status
DeletionSentForApproval, making the UI misleading during multi-step
deletion flows.

Fix: move the onSuccess call inside the ProcessingStatus.DeletedRiSc
branch so navigation only occurs when the RiSc is actually deleted.

[Mgmt Emitter] Fix cross-scope parent detection for LegacyOperations resources (#56800)

* [Mgmt Generator] Fix list operation routing for cross-scope parent resources

When a resource has a tenant-scoped list operation but a subscription-scoped
parent (or vice versa), the list operation was incorrectly routed to extension
methods instead of the collection. This happened because the list operation's
resourceScope (tenant path) didn't exactly match the parentResourceId
(subscription path).

Added a fallback check in CategorizeMethods: when the exact scope match fails,
verify if the operation path structurally matches the resource's ID pattern
minus the last segment (i.e., it IS a 'list children' path for the resource).
If so, route it to the collection where it belongs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cross-scope parent detection in emitter, not generator

Move the fix from generator routing (ResourceMetadataExtensions.cs) to the
emitter's postProcessArmResources. When LegacyOperations creates the same
model at multiple scopes (e.g., subscription and tenant), the path-based
parent detection in buildArmProviderSchema correctly identifies scope-matching
parents. However, postProcessArmResources was overwriting these with the first
decorator-based match (from @parentResource), which could pick the wrong scope.

The fix: skip overwriting parentResourceId in postProcessArmResources if it
was already set by the caller's path-based detection.

Added emitter test for cross-scope LegacyOperations parent assignment.
Reverted generator-side workaround (IsListChildrenPath).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use SQLITE_DB_PATH to avoid collision with Railway's DATABASE_URL

health checks to fail. Renamed to SQLITE_DB_PATH and defaulted to
/data/standupper.db to align with the Railway volume mount at /data.

New adversarial quality dimensions (measured per cycle by LLM agents):
- security: SAST + secrets scan + CVE deps
- architecture: coupling, complexity, SOLID
- no_slop: dead code, placeholders, copy-paste
- fallback: error handling, no silent failures
- honesty: comments match code, no lies
- mock_data: no hardcoded/fake data in prod
- hardcoded: no magic numbers, no hardcoded URLs/credentials
- test_quality: coverage ≥80%, real assertions, no false tests
- over_engineering: YAGNI, no premature abstraction
- observability: logging, metrics, health checks
- resilience: retry, timeout, graceful degradation

Mining redesign (#22)

* Add Health: 1000 at game start

Initialize player Health token to 1000 in GameState::new_with_rng(),
closing the health initialization gap. Previously Health was 0 until
the first combat encounter set it to 2000.

Update api_end_to_end test to expect Health=1000 instead of 2000.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix hardcoded card IDs in tests with dynamic API discovery

Replace hardcoded card IDs [9, 8, 10] in resolve_play_tests.rs and
flow_tests.rs with dynamic discovery via /library/cards?location=Hand
API queries. This makes tests resilient to card initialization changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* BREAKING: Redesign mining to light-level/yield mechanic

Implement roadmap 9.5 'Better Mining redesign':
- Add MiningLightLevel, MiningYield, MiningPower token types
- Redesign MiningCardEffect: fully token-based costs/gains instead of
  ore_damage/durability_prevent fields
- Redesign OreCard: token-based damages vector instead of single
  durability_damage field
- Redesign MiningDef: initial_light_level instead of OreHealth
- Redesign MiningEncounterState: simplified, no ore_tokens/rewards
- New player action EncounterConcludeEncounter with ConcludeEncounter
  payload, dispatching to conclude_mining_encounter()
- Mining cards: power cards (MiningPower gains), light cards
  (MiningLightLevel gains with cap, Lumber cost), stamina rest cards
- Ore deck: token-based damages (light, durability, rare health)
- Yield formula: mining_power × light_level / 100
- Conclude reward: min(stamina, yield) ore tokens, costs that stamina
- Encounter-scoped tokens cleaned up on encounter end

- scenario_mining_expansion_cards_exist: update count to 8 mining cards
- scenario_cost_mining_card_rejected_without_stamina: dynamic card discovery
- Add combat_encounter_ids() and mining_encounter_ids() dynamic helpers
- Fix abort path to call finish_mining_encounter for proper token cleanup

commit.message:
fix: resolve "Failed to update user" error in UserManagement (#77)

* Initial plan

* fix: resolve Failed to update user error in UserManagement

- Edge function: make auth.users metadata update non-fatal when only
  role/location/name are being synced; only abort on email/password
  auth failures. This prevents transient auth-API errors from blocking
  the public.users profile update (the source of truth).
- Edge function: add fallback error strings so error responses always
  carry a non-empty `error` field (avoids JSON.stringify dropping
  undefined values and returning `{}`).
- userService.updateUser: also check `result.message` as fallback so
  Supabase infrastructure errors (which use `message` not `error`) are
  surfaced correctly instead of falling back to the generic string.
- UserManagement.jsx: add editable `name` input to the inline edit row,
  include trimmed name in the save payload, add client-side validation
  for the required name field, and surface the real server error message
  instead of a hard-coded string. Refactor repeated editingUser?.id
  check into a local `isEditing` variable for clarity.

Co-authored-by: craigbfelt <77277252+craigbfelt@users.noreply.github.com>
